modules.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains
documentation: # file for chaining functions in chatbot

classes.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains.chain

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains.chain.invoke
documentation:
Invoke the chain
Returns:
    str: Output from the chain

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains.chain.chain_seqeunce_invoke
documentation:
Chain invoke the chain
Args:
    middle_chain (list): List of functions/Prompts/RunnableLambda to chain
    final_chain (RunnableLambda): Final chain to run. Default is self.output_parser
Returns:
    str: Output from the chain

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains.chain.chain_parrellel_invoke
documentation:
Chain invoke the chain #### better to use RunnableParallel outside the class
Args:
    parrellel_chain (list): List of functions/Prompts/RunnableLambda to chain 
Returns:
    str: Output from the chain

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.chains.chain.chain_branch_invoke
documentation:
Chain invoke the chain #### better to use RunnableBranch outside the class
Args:
    branch_chain (dict): Dictionary of functions/Prompts/RunnableLambda to chain 
Returns:
    str: Output from the chain
Class to chain functions in chatbot

Invoke a prompt

functions.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.load_env

Load environment variables from a file

    None

documentation:
Add an API key to the environment
Args:

    key (str): API key
Returns:
    None

functions.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.get_chatbot_openai
documentation:
Load the chatbot model from OpenAI
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatOpenAI: Chatbot model

functions.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.get_chatbot_anthropic
documentation:
Load the chatbot model from Anthropic
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatAnthropic: Chatbot model

functions.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.get_chatbot_google_generative_ai
documentation:
Load the chatbot model from Google Generative AI
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model 
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatGoogleGenerativeAI: Chatbot model

documentation:
Load the chatbot model from Ollama

    model_name (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)

Ask a question to the chatbot

documentation: Returns a boto3 client for S3

Function to load any LLM model

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.add_message
documentation:
Add a message to the conversation
Args:
    message (str): Message to add
Returns:
    str: Answer from the chatbot. Adds the message to the conversation also.

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.get_all_messages
documentation:
Get all messages from the conversation

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.get_last_message

Get the last message from the conversation

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.get_all_messages_content
documentation:
Get all messages from the conversation
Returns:
    list: List of messages

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.save_conversation
documentation:
Save the conversation to a file or s3
Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file. if none then it will save to the file_path given in the constructor
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (client, bucket)
Returns:
    bool: True if saved successfully

methods.name: mb_rag.chatbot.basic.conversation_model.load_conversation
documentation:
Load the conversation from a file or s3
Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (client, bucket)
Returns:
    list: List of messages

A class to represent a conversation model
Attributes:
    chatbot (ChatOpenAI): Chatbot model
    context (str): Context of the conversation
    question (str): Question to ask
    message_list (list): List of messages in the conversation
    file_path (str): Path to the conversation file (if s3_path then add s3_path='loc' and client and bucket)

modules.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings
documentation: # Function to generate embeddings for the RAG model

functions.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.get_rag_openai
documentation:
Load model from openai for RAG
Args:
    model_type (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatOpenAI: Chatbot model

functions.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.get_rag_ollama
documentation:
Load model from ollama for RAG
Args:
    model_type (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    OllamaEmbeddings: Embeddings model

functions.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.get_rag_anthropic
documentation:
Load the chatbot model from Anthropic
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatAnthropic: Chatbot model

functions.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.get_rag_google
documentation:
Load the chatbot model from Google Generative AI
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model 
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    ChatGoogleGenerativeAI: Chatbot model

functions.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.load_rag_model
documentation:
Load a RAG model from a given model name and type
Args:
    model_name (str): Name of the model. Default is openai.
    model_type (str): Type of the model. Default is text-embedding-ada-002.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments (temperature, max_tokens, timeout, max_retries, api_key etc.)
Returns:
    RAGModel: RAG model

classes.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.check_file
documentation: Check if the file exists

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.tokenize
documentation:
Function to tokenize the text
Args:
    text: text to tokenize
Returns:
    tokens

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.generate_text_embeddings
documentation:
Function to generate text embeddings
Args:
    text_data_path: list of text files
    # metadata: list of metadata for each text file. Dictionary format
    text_splitter_type: type of text splitter. Default is recursive_character
    chunk_size: size of the chunk
    chunk_overlap: overlap between chunks
    folder_save_path: path to save the embeddings
    replace_existing: if True, replace the existing embeddings
Returns:
    None

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.load_vectorstore
documentation:
Function to load vector store
Args:
    vector_store_type: type of vector store
Returns:
    vector store

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.load_embeddings
documentation:
Function to load embeddings from the folder
Args:
    embeddings_path: path to the embeddings
Returns:
    embeddings

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.load_retriever
documentation:
Function to load retriever
Args:
    embeddings_path: path to the embeddings
    search_type: list of str: type of search. Default : ["similarity_score_threshold"]
    search_params: list of dict: parameters for the search. Default : [{"k": 3, "score_threshold": 0.9}]
Returns:
    Retriever. If multiple search types are provided, a list of retrievers is returned

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.add_data
documentation:
Function to add data to the existing db/embeddings
Args:
    embeddings_path: path to the embeddings
    data: list of data to add
    text_splitter_type: type of text splitter. Default is recursive_character
    chunk_size: size of the chunk
    chunk_overlap: overlap between chunks
Returns:
    None

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.query_embeddings
documentation:
Function to query embeddings
Args:
    search_type: type of search
    query: query to search
Returns:
    results

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.get_relevant_documents
documentation:
Function to get relevant documents
Args:
    query: query to search
Returns:
    results

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.generate_rag_chain
documentation:
Function to start a conversation chain with a rag data. Call this to load a rag_chain module.
Args:
    context_prompt: prompt to context
    retriever: retriever. Default is None.
    llm: language model. Default is openai. Need chat model llm. "ChatOpenAI", "ChatAnthropic" etc. like chatbot
Returns:
    rag_chain_model.

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.conversation_chain
documentation:
Function to create a conversation chain
Args:
    query: query to search
    rag_chain : rag_chain model
    file: load a file and update it with the conversation. If None it will not be saved.
Returns:
    results

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.load_conversation
documentation:
Function to load the conversation
Args:
    file: file to load
    list_type: if True, return the chat_history as a list. Default is False.
Returns:
    chat_history

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.save_conversation
documentation:
Function to save the conversation
Args:
    chat: chat results
    file: file to save
Returns:
    None

methods.name: mb_rag.rag.embeddings.embedding_generator.firecrawl_web
documentation:
Function to get data from website. Use this to get data from a website and save it as embeddings/retriever. To ask questions from the website,
  use the load_retriever and query_embeddings function.
Args:
    website : str - link to website.
    api_key : api key of firecrawl, if None environment variable "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY" will be used.
    mode(str) : 'scrape' default to just use the same page. Not the whole website.
    file_to_save: path to save the embeddings
    **kwargs: additional arguments
Returns:
    retriever

Class to generate embeddings for the RAG model abnd chat with data
Args:
    model: type of model. Default is openai. Options are openai, anthropic, google, ollama
    model_type: type of model. Default is text-embedding-3-small. Options are text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-3-large, text-embedding-ada-002 for openai.
    vector_store_type: type of vector store. Default is chroma
    logger: logger
    model_kwargs: additional arguments for the model
    vector_store_kwargs: additional arguments for the vector store
    collection_name: name of the collection (default : test)

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.bounding_box.google_model
documentation:
Function to get google genai model 
Args:
    model (str): Model name
    api_key (str): API key. If None, it will be fetched from the environment variable GOOGLE_API_KEY
    **kwargs: Additional arguments
Returns:
    model (GenerativeModel): GenerativeModel object
genai.configure(api_key=os.environ.get("GOOGLE_API_KEY"))
model = genai.GenerativeModel(model_name="gemini-1.5-pro-latest")

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.bounding_box.generate_bounding_box
documentation:
Function to generate bounding boxes
Args:
    model (GenerativeModel): GenerativeModel object
    image_path (str): Image path
    prompt (str): Prompt
Returns:
    res (str): Result

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.bounding_box.add_bounding_box
documentation:
Function to add bounding box to image
Args:
    image_path (str): Image path
    bounding_box (dict): Bounding box
    label (str): Label
Returns:
    image (Image): Image with bounding box

modules.name: mb_rag.utils.extra
documentation: # extra functions for RAG's

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.extra.load_env_file
documentation:
Load environment variables from a .env file.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the .env file. Defaults to '.env'.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.extra.pdf_to_text
documentation: Extract text from a PDF file.

functions.name: mb_rag.utils.extra.convert_pdfs_in_folder
documentation:
Convert all PDF files in the given folder to text files.
Args:
    folder_path (str): Path to the folder containing the PDF files.
Returns:
    None
Example : convert_pdfs_in_folder('/folder_path') # folder_path is the path to the folder containing the PDF files.
The converted PDF files and text files will be created in the same folder
